crystal_ball.geometry: SphereGeometry(0.7, 8, 6) — 8 longitude × 6 latitude segments = readable low-poly facets
crystal_ball.material: MeshStandardMaterial transparent purple, roughness:0.25, metalness:0.1, opacity:0.82, emissive:0x6030aa, emissiveIntensity:0.6
crystal_ball.animated: sphere bobs ±0.04u at 0.9Hz; crystal point-light pulses 2.2±0.4 at 1.8Hz; emissive pulses 0.5±0.15 at 1.4Hz
crystal_ball.note: LairGLBAssets.ts has NO crystal ball GLB — 5 GLBs: cauldron_merlin, bougie, table_druidique, bibliotheque, sol_pierre, mur_pierre. Crystal stays procedural permanently.

breakdown.role: individual candle glow per CauldronSystem/candle logic

lighting_setup.assessment: Solid 3-pass (key/rim/fill) + ambient. Key light at y=8 creates harsh top-down shadows — Celtic dramaturgy is partial. No dedicated backlight for character depth. Fill intensity (0.4) is low but acceptable. Rim (0x6699cc cool blue) is good atmospheric contrast.
lighting_setup.dramaturgical_gap: Key light at [0,8,-2] is positioned directly overhead — creates flat mushroom shadows on back wall. A forward-angled fire key at [0,3,3] position (camera-side) would give more cinematic separation between foreground zones and back wall.

bookshelf_handler.code_path: main.ts line 342 — falls through to showZoneToast(zone)
bookshelf_handler.current_behavior: Shows toast: 'Journal de Merlin / Les pages sont encore en gestation'
bookshelf_handler.stub_label: Explicitly labelled as stub ('encore en gestation')
bookshelf_handler.assessment: Intentional placeholder — not a bug, but a missing feature. No crash, no blackout.

cauldron_handler.code_path: main.ts line 342 — falls through to showZoneToast(zone)
cauldron_handler.current_behavior: Shows toast: 'Chaudron Druidique / L\'anam doit d\'abord s\'éveiller'

showRunSummary_lore_risk.critical_issue: Line 108: always calls fadeIn(800) UNCONDITIONALLY — this triggers a full black overlay regardless of calling context
showRunSummary_lore_risk.if_called_from_lair: Would read store.getState() — state.run.biome/cardsDrawn/lifeCurrent etc. all reflect PREVIOUS run values (or defaults if no run completed). The overlay would show stale/zeroed stats. NOT safe to call from Lair context.
showRunSummary_lore_risk.safe_alternative: No existing variant without fadeIn. A non-destructive path would need to either: (a) read from a separate 'lastRunSummary' persisted object, or (b) gate on store.getState().run.status === 'complete' before calling.

aria_on_lair_wrapper.aria_attributes: NONE — wrapper created at main.ts line 221-224 with only id + cssText. No role, no aria-label.
aria_on_lair_wrapper.canvas_aria: renderer.domElement (THREE canvas) also has no aria-label or role attribute set in MerlinLairScene.ts initMerlinLair
aria_on_lair_wrapper.toast_aria: showZoneToast() correctly sets role=status, aria-live=polite, aria-atomic=true on each toast
aria_on_lair_wrapper.gap: The 3D canvas interactive region has no accessible name or role. Screen readers cannot identify it as a game scene or announce available interactions.

mobile_touch_gaps.passive_false: Both registered with { passive: false } — correct for preventDefault on scroll conflict
mobile_touch_gaps.gap_1: No touchend handler — finger-lift does NOT fire a hover-clear. On mobile, currentHovered stays visually active (emissive boosted) after the player lifts their finger.
mobile_touch_gaps.gap_2: touch-action CSS not set on the canvas — browser may still attempt scroll on the canvas element before passive:false fires on touchmove. Should add touch-action:none to renderer.domElement.style in initMerlinLair.

criteria_scores.assessment: All procedural geometry consistently uses flatShading:true. GLB assets (cauldron, bougie, table, shelf, walls, floor) loaded with roughness patching. Crystal ball correctly low-poly (8×6 sphere). Skull 8×7, candles 8-sided — coherent aesthetic throughout. Minor: GLB book material not explicitly validated for flatShading; Blender export may differ.

sol_pierre.glb positioned y=-4.98 (0.02 above procedural floor) to avoid z-fighting — good. mur_pierre tiles at z=-9.76 (0.02 in front of procedural wall at -9.74 mortar) — correct offset. Procedural floor/walls hidden on GLB load. Residual risk: 18 back-wall tiles share the same z-offset; at grazing angles tiling seams may shimmer. Not critical.

Strong material variety: crystal (transparent, emissive, pulsing), bones (matte), potions (glassy transparent), wood (rough brown). GLB cauldron materials cloned to prevent prototype pollution (C81-04). Gap: bookshelf book materials are created fresh per-book in a loop — 18 MeshStandardMaterial instances for 18 books (6 colors × 3 rows) never shared or disposed individually. Minor GC risk.

3-pass lighting (key/rim/fill) plus ambient is architecturally correct. Key light at [0,8,-2] is too high — creates flat overhead illumination rather than the warm forward-fire feel of a Celtic hall. Rim [8,2,7] blue-cool is effective. Fill left-wall [−9,0,−5] warm is correct. Missing: no dramatic low-angle backlight to silhouette props. Crystal + door flicker + candles add dynamism but the base key pass lacks depth.

5 of 6 major zone objects have GLB overlays (cauldron, candle, table, shelf, floor, walls). Crystal ball has NO GLB — permanently procedural. This is intentional per code comments but means the most visually prominent interactive prop (crystal) shows only a low-poly sphere. Acceptable as Celtic style but could be elevated.

Room: walls span y=-5 to ~11 (height 16), x-width ~23.5 units, depth ~9.75. Camera at z=7, looking at z=-4. FOV 62°. Table at z=-3 (foreground), crystal at z=-4 (mid), shelf at z=-8 (back). Depth staggering is good. Crystal pedestal at y=-2 with sphere at y=-1 — correct eyeline. Door at back wall. Minor: potion bottles at z=-7.5 to -7.8 may clip into the bookshelf at x=8,z=-8.

Color palette: dark brown/amber wood, purple crystal, gold toasts, Celtic-warm fog (#0d0a08) — coherent. Procedural assets use geometric low-poly Celtic craft aesthetic. Zone labels in French with druidic vocabulary ('Ogham runique', 'anam', 'druidique') — good lore coherence. Gap: bookshelf zone label 'Journal de Merlin' is a named stub ('Les pages sont encore en gestation') — breaks immersion; better to label as 'Bibliotheque Runique' with a non-stub hint.

Cannot measure directly from source — estimated from prior cycles. No new large dependencies added in C84/C84b. 18 per-book material instances noted above are minor. GLB files loaded async via loadGLB (cached by AssetLoader). No bloat regression detected from code audit. Score held from C84 estimate.

7 point lights in scene (4 base + crystal + door flicker + 3 candles = 8 total). Each PointLight with shadow disabled (no shadow maps). Animation: camera sway (2 sin), crystal (3 sin), door (1 sin), candles (updateCandles), dust (particle update), cauldron steam. No heavy post-processing. Risk: mur_pierre tiles add 48 clone() calls to scene graph (18 back + 15 left + 15 right). No batching — 48 draw calls for wall tiles alone. Mobile may drop below 30fps.

dispose() correctly traverses full scene, disposes geometry + material for all Mesh/Points. C81-03 lairDisposed flag prevents late GLB .then() from adding to torn-down scene. C81-04 GLB material clone prevents shared-ref mutation. Known residual: dispose() does not call renderer.dispose() or renderer.forceContextLoss() — renderer WebGL context stays alive after lair teardown. Second minor: the 3 bougie clones each have individually cloned materials (correct per C81 fix) but clone geometry is shared — dispose() will dispose the shared geometry multiple times (harmless but noisy).

CRITICAL GAP: The #lair-canvas-wrapper div has NO ARIA attributes (no role, no aria-label). The THREE.js canvas has no accessible description. Toasts correctly use role=status + aria-live=polite. Hover cursor change (pointer/default) is visual only — no accessible hover announcement. Interactive zones (5 zones) have no keyboard focus path — click-only. Zero keyboard navigation. Touch: no touchend hover-clear.

Crystal ball pulsing (3 simultaneous animations: bob, light pulse, emissive pulse) is excellent tactile feedback. Camera sway (0.3Hz x, 0.23Hz y) adds life without nausea risk. Door light flicker at 4.1Hz is appropriately rapid for fire. Candle flicker system. Dust motes particle system. Forest window with leaf sway + glass shimmer. Zone click SFX (C82) + hover shimmer SFX. Run summary (showRunSummary) calls fadeIn(800) unconditionally — if ever called from Lair context (bookshelf zone future feature) it would black-out the active 3D scene destructively. Bookshelf stub label 'en gestation' breaks 4th wall — minor.

cycle_score.note: Score dip is a measurement artifact — lighting_dramaturgie and fps_budget details now formally captured with precise deductions. No code regressions introduced in C84/C84b.

top2_priority_issues.title: Lair canvas wrapper has zero ARIA — screen reader invisible + no keyboard navigation
top2_priority_issues.root_cause: runMerlinLair() creates #lair-canvas-wrapper with only id+cssText. initMerlinLair() appends renderer.domElement with no accessible attributes. No keyboard focus path to any of the 5 interactive zones.
top2_priority_issues.impact: Blocks WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Zero keyboard users can interact with the Lair. Score contribution: 5.8/10 pulling the overall average down by ~0.16 points.

implementation.change_1: Add role='region' aria-label='Repaire de Merlin — scene interactive 3D' to wrapper element after creation.
implementation.change_2: In initMerlinLair() after renderer.domElement is appended, add: renderer.domElement.setAttribute('role','application'); renderer.domElement.setAttribute('aria-label','Scene 3D interactive — 5 zones: carte, cristal, etagere, chaudron, porte'); renderer.domElement.setAttribute('tabindex','0');
implementation.change_3: In initMerlinLair() add keydown listener on renderer.domElement: Tab cycles focus through the 5 zone names announced via aria-live region; Enter fires zoneClickCallback on focused zone. This is the minimum keyboard path.
implementation.change_4: In onTouchMove/onTouchStart add a touchend listener that calls applyHoverTo(currentHovered, baseEmissive) and sets currentHovered = null — clears hover state on finger lift.

top2_priority_issues.title: Key light is directly overhead — flat mushroom shadows kill Celtic dramaturgy
top2_priority_issues.root_cause: setupLighting() places the key PointLight at [0, 8, -2]. Camera is at z=7 looking at z=-4. The key is 10 units behind the camera's focal point — effectively a top-down fixture. This projects shadows straight down, flattening all geometry and eliminating the warmth of a firelit Celtic interior.
top2_priority_issues.impact: Reduces perceived visual quality for ALL zone objects simultaneously. A Celtic hall should feel lit by torches at human height (y=1.5 to 3.0), not overhead ceiling fixtures. Score: 6.5/10 — lowest non-ARIA criterion.

implementation.change_1: Move key light from [0, 8, -2] to [0, 3.0, 2] — forward-angled fire key now throws shadows backward into scene depth, illuminating the near-zone props (crystal, cauldron) while leaving back wall in relative shadow.
implementation.change_2: Reduce key intensity from 1.1 to 0.9 (forward key is closer to subject, same energy budget).
implementation.change_3: Add a LOW backlight PointLight at [0, 1.5, -11] color 0xff5500 intensity 0.35 range 14 decay 2 — simulates torchlight bouncing off the back stone wall, creating a subtle warm rim on back-scene objects (bookshelf, cauldron). This costs one additional draw call but is small.
implementation.change_4: Optional: increase fill from 0.4 to 0.55 at same position [-9, 0, -5] to compensate for key being pulled forward (less fill spillover on left wall needed when key is forward).
implementation.risk: LOW — lighting is pure additive; no logic affected. Can be tuned visually in browser.

additional_findings.title: renderer.dispose() not called in Lair dispose()
additional_findings.detail: dispose() traverses scene and disposes all geometry/materials but never calls renderer.dispose(). The WebGL context stays alive. On browsers with strict WebGL context limits (Safari iOS: max 8), repeated lair init/dispose cycles may exhaust contexts.
additional_findings.recommendation: Add renderer.dispose() as the last line of the dispose() function in MerlinLairScene.ts.

additional_findings.title: mur_pierre wall tiles: 48 independent draw calls (no batching)
additional_findings.detail: loadLairGLBs adds 48 cloned tiles (18 back + 15 left + 15 right) as separate scene objects. Each is a separate draw call. THREE.InstancedMesh or merging geometries post-clone would reduce to 3 draw calls (one per wall face).
additional_findings.recommendation: For C86+, consider THREE.InstancedMesh for tile walls. Not blocking for C85.

additional_findings.title: showRunSummary() cannot be safely called from Lair context
additional_findings.detail: The function unconditionally calls fadeIn(800) and reads live run state. If bookshelf zone is wired to show run history in a future cycle, a new showLairJournal() function should be created that reads from a persisted lastRun object (not live state) and skips fadeIn.
additional_findings.recommendation: When bookshelf zone is implemented (C87+), do NOT reuse showRunSummary(). Create showLairJournal() reading from store.getState().profile.lastRunSummary.

additional_findings.title: touch-action:none not set on canvas
additional_findings.detail: renderer.domElement has no touch-action:none CSS. On Android Chrome, momentum scrolling can compete with touchmove handler even with passive:false.

verdict.rationale: C85-01 (ARIA) alone would raise zone_feedback_aria from 5.8 to ~8.5 (+2.7 criterion points / +0.225 average). C85-02 (lighting) would raise lighting_dramaturgie from 6.5 to ~8.0 (+1.5 criterion / +0.125 average). Combined projected score after both fixes: 7.44 + 0.225 + 0.125 = ~7.79 (matches C84 level). To reach 8.0 both fixes must land PLUS renderer.dispose() (C85-03) for memory_leaks boost to 8.5 (+0.025 avg). Achievable in C85.
verdict.next_cycle_recommendation: After C85 fixes land, C86 should target: mur_pierre InstancedMesh batching (fps_budget: 7.5→8.5), and crystal ball GLB or SphereGeometry upgrade to ico (IcosahedronGeometry detail=2 for true Celtic gem feel) to raise glb_coverage + material_quality.
